title: achyranthes aspera var. indica
intro: Achyranthes aspera var. indica, also known as the Indian spiny chaff flower or Aspera, is a plant species within the Achyranthes genus, native to various parts of Asia, including India, China, and Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits of Achyranthes aspera var. indica include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and alleviating joint pain and arthritis. Achyranthes aspera var. indica is often prepared as a herbal decoction, tincture, or powder for medicinal use, with the dried roots and leaves being commonly used in various herbal preparations.
